deployment_notes: CPU-only 1U server, typically used as cluster management, login, or data nodes
No GPU slots -- not suitable for GPU workloads
Commonly deployed alongside GPU nodes (AS-8125GS-TNHR) in ORI-TX clusters

related_variants.difference: 8U GPU server with 8x GPU slots -- the GPU compute counterpart to this CPU node

changes: Production BIOS for Intel Xeon LGA 4677

notes: MAAS power_type 'ipmi' works for power on/off/cycle despite the BMC reporting AccountTypes=['Redfish'] only.
The BMC does NOT accept IPMI as an AccountType value (PATCH returns 400), but IPMI commands still function correctly over the network.
If MAAS reports 'Unable to power control the node', the cause is almost always MAAS database contention (500 transaction errors), NOT an IPMI/Redfish issue. Retry after load subsides.
The 'maas' BMC account role may be reset to 'Operator' by MAAS commissioning. This is normal and does not break IPMI power control.
Redfish power control (redfish_set_nextboot with reboot=true) always works as a fallback to verify BMC health.
known_false_alarms: maas_check_bmc_health reports 'AccountTypes=[Redfish] -- missing IPMI' as an issue. On SYS-121H-TNR this is cosmetic; IPMI power control works regardless.
maas_check_bmc_health reports 'Role mismatch: BMC RoleId=Operator but MAAS privilege_level=OPERATOR'. The role name casing differs but the privilege is correct.
troubleshooting: If MAAS deploy fails with 'Unable to power control the node': 1) Check MAAS event log for 500 errors (DB contention), 2) Verify BMC responds via redfish_get_info, 3) Test power control via redfish_set_nextboot, 4) Retry deploy after MAAS DB load subsides.
If power_state shows 'off' but MAAS thinks it's 'on': Use redfish_get_info to check actual PowerState, then use redfish_set_nextboot to correct.

notes: Model: 1=1U form factor, 2=2.5" drive, 1=single socket, H=Intel Xeon
Used as CPU/management nodes in ORI-TX data center alongside GPU nodes
BMC firmware 01.07.01 does not support adding 'IPMI' to AccountTypes via Redfish PATCH (returns 400), but IPMI works natively
MAAS v3.3.x has known DB contention issues under heavy load (many simultaneous disk erases) that manifest as power control failures -- not a BMC issue

known_configurations.name: ORI-TX CPU Node (dfw01-cpu-08 through dfw01-cpu-11)
known_configurations.notes: Deployed at ORI-TX. MAAS managed via IPMI power type. BMC AccountTypes=['Redfish'] only but IPMI works. ConnectX-7 NICs (MCX75310AAS-NEAT) with firmware 28.43.2026.
